source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a stiffened aluminum-alloy hull bottom plating panel in the forward region of a 15 m high-speed planing craft (design speed 35 knots, full-load displacement 15 metric tons) per ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft Part 3 (2013), delivered as a multi-part assembly because the ABS scantling equations and FEM pass/fail all reference the coupled behavior of plating, longitudinal stiffeners, and transverse frames connected by welded joints. ABS Part 3 Section 2 defines slamming design pressure; Section 3 gives minimum plating thickness; Section 4 gives stiffener scantlings; Section 3.5 gives fatigue at welded joints.

Assembly layout. (A) Hull bottom plating: Al 5083-H116, 500 mm (between longitudinals) x 1200 mm (between transverse frames) panel at Station 4 (forward of midships, x/L = 0.3), thickness 3-8 mm design variable (trial 5 mm). (B) Longitudinal stiffeners: aluminum L-bar 50x50x5 mm, running fore-aft, welded to plate inner face at 500 mm pitch. (C) Transverse frames: aluminum T-section 80x40x6 mm, running athwartships, welded to plate and longitudinals at 1200 mm pitch. (D) Welded joints: MIG welds at plate-to-longitudinal and plate-to-frame intersections, fatigue Class D per ABS appendix.

Material. Al 5083-H116 marine plate/extrusion per Aluminum Association and ABS: E = 70 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2700 kg/m^3, Fty = 215 MPa (welded minimum), Ftu = 305 MPa. ABS permissible stress sigma_perm = 120 MPa for welded aluminum (Part 3 Table 3.2).

Design pressures (ABS HSC Part 3 Section 2). For a 15 m craft at 35 knots, design vertical acceleration a_v = 6 g at forward stations. Slamming design pressure at Station 4 bottom: p_sl_bottom = 50 kPa. Hydrostatic+hydrodynamic service pressure p_design = 40 kPa. Fatigue range reference 20 kPa over 10^6 cycles.

Load cases. LC1 (slamming 50 kPa uniform on panel, stiffener-simply-supported). LC2 (hydrostatic 40 kPa). LC3 (slamming + hull-girder bending contribution 60 MPa). LC4 (fatigue 10^6 cycles @ 20 kPa range, Class D S-N at welded toe).

Pass/fail criteria (all on the assembled plate+stiffener+frame panel). (R1) Plating thickness t satisfies the ABS HSC Section 3.3 scantling equation t >= 5 mm for this panel geometry and slamming pressure (note ABS coefficient form). (R2) Under LC1 slamming, plating max von Mises <= sigma_perm = 120 MPa. (R3) Under LC3 slamming + hull-girder, plating max von Mises <= Fty = 215 MPa (ABS ultimate). (R4) Panel mid-point deflection under LC2 <= s/300 = 1.67 mm (ABS serviceability). (R5) Longitudinal stiffener section modulus Z >= 25 cm^3 per ABS HSC Part 3 Section 4 scantling (L-bar 50x50x5 has Z ~ 8 cm^3, so the trial stiffener is binding and must be upsized). (R6) Fatigue weld-toe stress range at 10^6 cycles <= 71 MPa (ABS Class D S-N). (R7) Panel plating + longitudinals + half-frame mass per unit hull area <= 25 kg/m^2.

Assembly policy. Plating stress (R2, R3) depends on the stiffener pitch (A-B) and frame pitch (A-C) boundary conditions. Stiffener section modulus (R5) is a property of the B element welded to the A plate via flange-plate composite section. Weld-toe fatigue (R6) exists only at the A-B and A-C welded interfaces. None of the pass/fail can be evaluated on plating-alone, stiffener-alone, or frame-alone geometry.

Solver and analysis expectations. Primary: linear static FEM with shell plating + beam/shell stiffeners (10 mm mesh in plating, finer at weld toes). Closed-form ABS scantling cross-check for R1/R5. Hot-spot stress fatigue at welded toes for R6. Buckling, modal, thermal, fluid CFD, radiation analyses NOT required.

Deliverables. STEP AP242 of stiffened panel assembly, mass per unit hull area in kg/m^2, plating thickness, max von Mises per LC, mid-point deflection, stiffener Z actual vs required, and hot-spot weld-toe stress range.
short_prompt:
Design a stiffened aluminum-alloy hull bottom plating panel in the forward region of a 15 m high-speed planing craft (35 knots, 15 metric tons full-load) per ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft Part 3 (2013). Delivered as a multi-part assembly: Al 5083-H116 plating (500 mm between longitudinals x 1200 mm between transverse frames at Station 4, x/L = 0.3, trial 5 mm thickness) + aluminum L-bar 50 x 50 x 5 mm longitudinals (500 mm pitch) + aluminum T-section 80 x 40 x 6 mm transverse frames (1200 mm pitch) + MIG welds (fatigue Class D per ABS appendix).

Material. Al 5083-H116: E = 70 GPa, nu = 0.33, rho = 2700 kg/m^3, Fty = 215 MPa (welded minimum), Ftu = 305 MPa. ABS permissible stress sigma_perm = 120 MPa for welded aluminum (Part 3 Table 3.2).

Design pressures (ABS HSC Part 3 Section 2). Design vertical acceleration a_v = 6 g forward. Station 4 slamming p_sl_bottom = 50 kPa; hydrostatic+hydrodynamic service p_design = 40 kPa; fatigue range 20 kPa over 10^6 cycles.

Load cases.
- LC1 slamming 50 kPa uniform on panel.
- LC2 hydrostatic 40 kPa.
- LC3 slamming + hull-girder bending contribution 60 MPa.
- LC4 fatigue 10^6 cycles at 20 kPa range, Class D weld toe.

Pass limits: LC1 von Mises <= 120 MPa; LC3 <= 215 MPa; LC2 mid-point deflection <= s/300 = 1.67 mm; stiffener Z >= 25 cm^3 (L-bar 50 x 50 x 5 gives ~8 cm^3, so stiffener must be upsized); Class D hot-spot <= 71 MPa; mass <= 25 kg/m^2.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Plate thickness and stiffener section-modulus equations are mandated closed-form formulas.

prompt.title: 5083-H116 stiffened bottom hull panel per ABS HSC Part 3 as multi-part assembly
prompt.brief: 500x1200 mm welded stiffened panel (plating + longitudinals + transverse frames) Station 4, slamming 50 kPa, hull-girder 60 MPa, ABS welded allowable 120 MPa; fatigue 10^6 at Class D.

parts.name: bottom_plating
parts.role: Al 5083-H116 bottom plate panel between stiffeners and frames

parts.name: longitudinal_stiffener
parts.role: L-bar longitudinal welded to plate inner face carrying longitudinal bending of panel

parts.name: transverse_frame
parts.role: T-section transverse frame welded across longitudinals providing panel shortspan support

boundary_conditions.panel_edges: simply supported at stiffeners and frames

governing_standard.name: ABS Rules for Building and Classing High-Speed Craft Part 3

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: ABS HSC Part 3 Section 3.3 minimum-thickness equation
ABS HSC Part 3 Section 4 stiffener scantling
ABS Class D S-N fatigue at 10^6 cycles

interfaces.mediated_by: MIG fillet weld (ABS Class D at weld toe)
interfaces.load_path: transverse and longitudinal shear transfer; fatigue hot-spot at weld toe
short-span support; fatigue hot-spot at weld toe
load sharing between longitudinal and transverse grids

fem_check_policy.rationale: Plating stress (R2, R3) depends on stiffener+frame pitch; stiffener section modulus (R5) is a composite (B flange + A plate) property; weld-toe fatigue (R6) exists only at welded interfaces. No individual part has a meaningful pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part_is_inherent: ABS HSC scantling equations and fatigue rules are interface-specific; a bare unstiffened plate or a lone stiffener does not constitute the panel the Rules refer to.

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Global hull-girder vibration out of scope.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Panel buckling not driving at this station; could be production concern.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Global FE hull model per ABS HSC Part 3 Section 1
Galvanic corrosion between Al plate and steel fasteners

eval_coverage: ABS scantling closed-form + FEM stress on assembled stiffened panel
slamming + hull-girder bending combined across interfaces
welded-joint S-N fatigue at explicit weld-toe locations
